item.title: base gravy
item.description:
#ingredients
This is basically a stock for curries to add volume to the curry.
Just make the 30 minute version and freeze it
This version is enough for 5 curries
#ingredients

75ml oil
500-600g onions
1/4 green pepper
1/2 tsp salt
1 1/2 tbsp ginger/garlic paste
2 tbsp tomato puree
1 tbsp mix powder
1/2 tsp garam masala
800ml water
Warning Needs to be diluted to consistency of semi-skimmed milk

item.title: basil
basmati rice
item.description:
1 cup rice, 1 3/4 cup water
Add 1 tsp of salt and a tbsp of oil
Or boil in water, drain and put lid back on for 5-10 mins to steam

item.title: bay leaf
BBQ sauce
beef
beef stock
bicarb of soda
item.description:
Base ingredient
Use for velveting meat
Also speeds up cooking of beans and polenta
Or try a pinch in roast potatoes for a 5 min boil

item.description:
Warning Buy it in paper, not the cans!
The paper cartons do not have to be heated as much in processing

item.title: coconut oil
coconut yoghurt
corn on the cob
corn tortillas
item.description:
Videos on how to make from fresh:
https://youtu.be/fKX7qHCpcw4
https://youtu.be/3J9mqTC6MnM

Heat up store bought:

Microwave in a wet palaver towel for 30-60 seconds
Put in tin foil to continue steaming for at least 5 mins (up to 15 mins)
Take out and place two on top on the meat to steam again
Take off and fry in hot oil while melting the cheese
Top with meat and toppings
Eat immediately

Best method by Rick Bayless using a microwave. Or this video
much softer than frying and they have a pocket of steam in the middle
Frying doesn’t work for me but could try 25 seconds, 30 seconds, flip again for 20 seconds to puff up from this video. You need to dip in water first, to get the steam going
Ethan

Put in tin foil/food bag to steam and keep warm

item.description: 2 parts cornstarch for 1 part water

item.title: salt
sausage
Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients


#ingredients

Couple sausages
chopped tomatoes
1/2 onion
carrot (optional)
celery (optional)
butter beans
chicken stock cube
1/2 tsp smoked paprika



Brown sausage
Sweat soffritto
Add liquids and smoked paprika
Cook on low heat for an hour
ONLY ADD BEANS 10 minutes from the end

item.title: scallops
item.description:
At Queen’s Park farmers market, £48/kg
So £9 for 6

Uncover to dry out and season
Ripping hot pan
Add high smoke point oil
When smoking add scallops and press down and season this side too
Cook for 90 secs ish
Flip and cook for 60 secs
54C inside
Warning Don’t use a stainless steel pan!
Don’t add too much oil

item.title: sea salt
Seasonal foods
item.description:
Type this up
Have a note per ingredient which says when it is in season
Can then see which recipes use similar ingredients, or if you have left over fennel or something you can see what to do with it

glass containers
freezer containers for a single portion of left overs

chocolate

this is not tomato puree
It is either:

tomato puree diluted with 3 times as much water
Blended chopped tomatoes

item.title: tomato puree
Growing
item.description:
Same as Cherry tomatoes for season

We have a determinate tomato

item.title: Tortillas
truffle
item.description:
Different types of truffle
I dont like summer truffles, they don’t taste of much and the texture is weird. I think that’s the same as Tartufo Estivo o Scorzone, which tastes more like a mushroom. Not sure, but some restaurants had truffle that didn’t taste like anything to me, all the ones in Umbria did
Need to try Tartufo Nero Pregiato from Norcia, Umbria in Mid March or November

za'tar

item.description:
#ingredients
I like Ethan Chlebowski recipe recipe. He says that roast garlic is nicer and butter is a better emulsifier



#ingredients

pasta for one person
1 garlic cloves
1 roasted head of garlic
2 tbsp olive oil
1 tbsp lemon juice
chilli flakes
Cherry tomatoes (optional)
Prawns (optional)

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients #ingredients #ingredients #ingredients

by Kenji


Steak marinade
#ingredients

300g skirt steak (or rump)
1 tsp light soy sauce
2 tsp Shaohsing wine
1/4 tsp MSG
Pinch of salt (used to be 1/2 tsp salt)
1 tsp cornstarch (apparently this is called cornflour in the UK)
Tenderstem broccoli
1/4 tsp bicarb of soda
Sauce
#ingredients

1 tbsp light soy sauce
1 tbsp dark soy sauce
1 tbsp oyster sauce
1 tbsp sugar
Cornstarch slurry
#ingredients

2 tsp cornstarch
1 tbsp water
Aromatics
#ingredients

1 garlic clove
Recipe says ginger but don’t put too much, none if Liv eating

item.title: Beef roast
item.description:
#ingredients

Adam Ragusea


#ingredients

Any beef joint
garlic
rosemary or thyme
butter
2 cups (400 ml) beef stock
1/2 cup (100 ml) red wine (optional)
1 tablespoons dried mushroom powder
1 tablespoons onion powder
1/2 teaspoon mustard powder
2 teaspoons cornstarch

item.title: Bolognese
item.description:
#ingredients
Try these ratios from NYTimes which is the same as this

from Kenji


#ingredients

2 tablespoons olive oil
1 red onion, very finely diced
2 celery sticks, very finely diced
1 large carrot, very finely diced
500g minced pork
500g minced beef (or lamb mince)
1/2 pack of pancetta (unsmoked)
50ml white wine
50ml milk
4 tablespoons tomato puree
300ml passata
400ml chicken stock
parmesan
Cheddar
based on one from Gino I think

item.title: Braised short rib
item.description:
#ingredients

Recipes by both Ethan Chlebowski and Adam Ragusea
have with:

cheesy polenta
cabbage


#ingredients

5 Beef short ribt on the bone
1/2 red onion, rough dice
1 carrot, rough dice
1 stalk of celery, rough dice
30 g tomato puree
750ml white wine (or a bit less with the stock)
200ml beef stock (or plain water or more wine)
2 bay leaf
Salt
30g oil


salt beef over night
brown to make a fond
add in mirepoux and cook for 10 minutes
add tomato puree and cook until browned (not burnt)
deglaze with wine
cook on low for 5 hours

item.title: Burger
item.description:
#ingredients
Or look at Smash burger
I have just included the main ingredients, you can customise obviously
Success Add suet to the burger to made it extra fatty
Variations:

in n out



#ingredients

burgers (or minced beef)
bread buns
onion
butter
cheese
Try this burger seasoning:

10 parts salt
4 parts MSG
2 parts black pepper
2 parts garlic powder
2 parts onion powder

item.title: Ingredients

#ingredients

1.6kg whole chicken
450ml buttermilk
Large plastic sealable bag
2 tbsp Salt

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients


#ingredients

burrata or mozzarella
tomatoes
basil
olive oil

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients

#ingredients

spaghetti or the shape from Trattoria Degli Umbri
2 egg yolks
Smoked thick cut bacon or pancetta
25g Parmesan
25g pecorino
black pepper



Start cooking bacon first on low heat
Boil pasta and save some water
Mix Parmesan and egg yolks to get a paste
Heat up very slowly to melt the cheese but not cook the egg. Wait until you can’t see bits of cheese then STOP (should be very liquidy still)
Last time it was watery after adding pasta water but suddenly turned, be careful
Put pasta into the eggs with pasta water
Add bacon and black pepper at the end

item.title: Carnitas
item.description:
#ingredients
I tried this one, but pork didn’t get that tender video
Going to try Rick Bayless
This is a make ahead recipe that can be turned into loads of different stuff (Idea from Ethan Chlebowski):

pork carnitas tacos
bbq pork bun
pork broccolli pasta
Also gives you pork fat to use in Egg fried rice


#ingredients

pork shoulder
salt

item.title: Cauliflower cheese
item.description:
#ingredients

This recipe is from On the side
Then also make cauliflower greens
Boil cauliflower instead of roasting first. Nicer texture and cleaner flavour


#ingredients

1 cauliflower
20g butter
20g flour
Milk
Cheese

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients
The quick roasting tin


#ingredients

1 cauliflower
250g kale
400g creme fraiche
1 egg
3 tsp Dijon mustard
80g Cheddar
200g feta
50 panko
baguette to serve

#ingredients

Left over cauliflower greens
1 tbsp oil
1/2 tbsp cider vinegar
1/2 tsp sugar
Salt

item.title: Chinese pork dumpling
item.description:
#ingredients
The Wok
Can make with dumpling dough or just buy dumpling wrappers
Based on this Yt short


#ingredients

dumpling dough (half what it says in The Wok)
1/2 pack of minced pork (220g ish)
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp MSG
1/2 tsp sugar
1 tsp oyster sauce
3 tsp light soy sauce
1/3 tsp white pepper
1 tsp cornstarch
ginger
spring onion (large chunks for water )
5 tsp green part of spring onion
3 tsp boiling Vegetable oil

item.guid: Recipes/Crispy baked cod with broccoli, peas and beans.md

item.title: dan Dan chicken sandwich
item.description:
#ingredients
Reading Max’s sandwich book and seeing how you just need to follow some basic principles to create a new sandwich
e.g. like he does in this video
So can I use sesame paste and Dan Dan noodles flavour but in a sandwich?

Hot = chicken breast or minced pork topping
Cold = cucumber? Or slaw
Sweet =
Sour = Sichuan peppercorns
Crunchy = crispy onions or peanuts or
Soft = sesame paste


#ingredients

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients



#ingredients

100g of egg noodles or spaghetti (if spaghetti then add 1 tsp bicarb of soda to get the texture)
1 tsp sesame paste (with extra oil to thin out)
1 tsp toasted sesame oil
1 tbsp light soy sauce
Dash of dark soy sauce
2 tbsp sichuan chilli oil (1.5 tbsp chiu chow chilli oil) and 1 tbsp sediment
4 Sichuan peppercorns (ground)

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients
Takes a lot of effort, might make it one day


#ingredients

Low salt chicken stock - might be hard to find might have to make chicken stock myself
gelatin
light soy sauce
tomato puree
onion powder

item.description:
#ingredients
Link


#ingredients

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients
Short


#ingredients

1 tbsp soy sauce (sweet variety is better)
1 tbsp Black vinegar
Few drops of toasted sesame oil
Few drops of chiu chow chilli oil

I think it needs salt but check as might change texture

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients



#ingredients

2 cups dry rice
2 eggs (don’t salt)
spring onion (or peas)
2 tbsp light soy sauce
1/4 tsp MSG
1/2 tsp salt (or 1/4 salt 1/4 tsp chicken powder)
1/2 tsp sugar
1 garlic clove
sesame oil to finish

item.title: katsu
item.description:

don’t cover with sauce, moisture is the enemy of crispiness

panko
flour
1 egg per 2 chicken breast
Korean chicken sauce, or any dipping sauce

item.title: Lamb burger
item.description:
#ingredients
From here Ethan Chlebowski



#ingredients

454 g (1 lb) boneless lamb leg or lamb stew meat or ground lamb
8 g salt (1.5 tsp)
15 g (2 tbsp) cornstarch
30 g ( 2 tbsp) neutral oil for wok frying
1 spring onion, sliced
Knob of ginger, grated
2 garlic cloves, grated
10 g (2 tbsp) ground cumin seeds
5 g (1 tbsp) Chinese dry red chile powder (substitute: cayenne pepper)
1/4 red onion, sliced
1 longhorn or thai chile pepper, sliced (optional)
Pinch of MSG (optional)

item.description:
#ingredients
Adam Ragusea

1.5 lb (680g) potatoes (any kind but I prefer waxy)
1 32 oz (946mL) carton of stock (I use chicken stock but any kind is fine)
1 cup (237mL) cream
salt
black pepper
fresh rosemary and thyme (or whatever herbs you've got)

item.title: Pasta alla Norcina
item.description:
#ingredients

from here
Basically sausage and mushroom creamy pasta


#ingredients

4 ounces baby Bella mushroom diced
0.5 pound bulk mild Italian sausage
¼ cup olive oil
1/2 small onion diced
1.5 cloves garlic
1/4 cup dry white wine
0.75 cups cups heavy cream
2 cups reserved pasta water will not need it all
1 pinch nutmeg
¾ cup pecorino Romano grated
salt and black pepper to taste

35 g extra virgin olive oil
14 g butter, softened to room temperature

item.title: Pizza
item.description:
#ingredients
Can keep in fridge or freeze
Adam Ragusea recipe

use left overs like
Or make something mental like this mortadella burrata sandwich


#ingredients

2 1/4 cups (530 ml) warm water
1 tbsp sugar (12g)
1 tbsp (9g) active dry yeast
2 tbsp (30 ml) olive oil
1 tbsp (18 g) kosher salt
5 cups (600g) bread flour, plus more for working the dough additional oil for greasing the dough
cornmeal, semolina flour, or coarse-ground whole wheat flour for dusting
170-225 g per pizza, low moisture mozzarella

item.title: polenta
item.description:
#ingredients

fine cornmeal (polenta)

item.title: pork carnitas tacos
item.description:

item.title: risotto

#ingredients
Adam Ragusea
You don't need to constantly stir


#ingredients

160g Arborio risotto rice
400ml chicken stock
shallots (optional)
garlic (optional)
Parmesan
Cheddar mozzarella mix (Liv)
If making with braised asparagus then boils trimmings and shavings with stock for 1 min to give extra flavour. Like in this video

item.title: Roast chicken dinner
item.description:
#ingredients
Adam Ragusea
says roast in a stainless steel pan!
Success The gravy from this is amazing too
Throw in some unpeeled garlic and shallots like Nigella - How to eat for some extra flavour
Leftovers could be:

Chicken, leek and chorizo pie
Chicken pot pie
Egg fried rice
One pot creamy chicken spinach (orzo)


#ingredients

whole chicken
potatoes
peas
lemon
shallot
garlic

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients


#ingredients

spice tailor butter chicken
fatty lamb like neck or shoulder
Brown lamb then follow instructions for curry
Put in slow cooker for 3 hours, should have tons of lamb fat that changes the flavour completely.

item.title: Steak
item.description:
#ingredients





Salt meat 40-60 mins before hand. Pat down before frying
Heat pan really hot
Place steaks and don’t move for 1.5-2 mins
Flip and another 1.5-2 mins
Don’t cut up meat for thin steaks as it goes cold
add finishing salt to cut meat
Bon appetite


This is traditional but is hard to execute
2-3 mins each side then oven for 6 mins (like Pan fried Duck breast)

Start off cold and keep flipping

Put in oven first on really low temperature (like 120C, which makes the temp even throughout
Take out when around 50C
sear for flavour



#ingredients

rib eye steak
Garlic
Butter
Cherry tomatoes

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients
Or just use the one in Chicken tacos


#ingredients

2 tablespoons chilli
1½ teaspoons ground cumin seeds
1 teaspoon kosher salt, or to taste
1 teaspoon black pepper, to taste
1 teaspoon cornstarch
1 teaspoon smoked paprika (or substitute hot or sweet paprika)
¼ teaspoon garlic powder
¼ teaspoon onion powder
½ teaspoon crushed chilli flakes, or to taste

item.title: Ingredients
item.description:
#ingredients
To use it on Salmon, cook as normal skin side down then flip over and add Teriyaki to pan and baste


#ingredients

1 tbsp sake
1 1/2 tbsp light soy sauce
1 1/2 tbsp water
2 tsp brown sugar
2 tsp honey
1/2 tsp cornstarch slurry (not 1/2tsp of cornstarch)

item.title: Thai basil stir fry
item.description:
#ingredients

Original recipe uses holy basil which is actually more similar to Italian basil than Thai basil


#ingredients

2 cloves garlic
1 Thai chilies, or 1 tbsp yellowbird serrano
3 mild red chilies
1/2 pack minced beef or minced pork (fatty)
2 teaspoons Fish sauce
1 tsp sugar
1 tsp chicken powder
1/2 cup basil
Jasmine rice
1 egg

item.title: Thai green curry
item.description:
#ingredients
Tip Make two of ice cups of jasmine rice and cool overnight for Egg fried rice the next day
And use left over curry with rice noodles!!
Best recipe I've seen is this one

it’s all about the kaffir lime leaves leaves


#ingredients

4-5 kaffir lime leaves leaves
Handful of thai basil
thai green curry paste about 2 tbsp (3 was too hot )
400ml coconut milk
coconut oil
2 chicken breast
bell pepper
1 tbsp fish sauce
1.5 tbsp palm sugar (or brown sugar) (add 1 more if needed)
1 cup chicken stock (use half a stock gel) (unsalted) if not then go easy on fish sauce
Jasmine rice or Thai sticky rice
Can also add courgette if you have any - add near the end for a nice crunch

item.title: Thai sticky rice
item.description:
#ingredients
Hot Thai kitchen
When done, take it out and massage it then put in a bamboo basket
“how-to-make-sticky-rice-khao-niew-10.jpg” could not be found.


#ingredients

160g sticky rice

item.title: Pasta
Lunch
Cuisine
Healthy
Meat
Quick
slow
Veggie
Meat free Monday
item.description:
Spring Gnocchi bake



Steak

item.title: Cooking as a database
item.description:
#ingredients
Welcome to my cooking vault!
On this public version you can find my collection of recipes, ingredients, and cookbooks (inspired by Frank's cooking). This is just the public facing arm of a larger Obsidian vault where I manage my grocery list (see this guide) and keep track of restaurants I liked (or hated so much it was noteworthy).

The beauty of using Obsidian for this kind of thing is that I can attach metadata to each recipe or ingredient note. This allows me to query notes as if they were in a big database, which can be used to look at my cooking knowledge from several different angles
For example, I can see all my recipes for a certain cuisine:
Japanese


all the lunch recipes under 20 mins long:

or I can plan a meal around a single ingredient in my fridge:
asparagus



When making a recipe the creator will try and find the combination of ingredients that will work best for most people. But I am not cooking for most people, I am cooking for myself.
The internet is full of different ways to achieve the same end goal. Its always funny to see one youtube video say "you must absolutely avoid this" after just seeing the same tip in another video. After trying to recipe out for myself, I will include a embedded link to my favourite website and write warnings to myself if I messed something up. In this way, I can keep building on my skills and technique and not accidentally create a chemical weapon by putting 3 tbsp, instead of 3 tsp, of fish sauce in my Thai green curry.

How many times have you taken a picture of a restaurant dish and left if lost in your cluttered camera roll? Since Obsidian keeps track of all the links between files, I can have a dataview query to show all the times I have had this meal in a restaurant. That way I can judge if I have hit RQ (restaurant quality).

Each recipe has its own list of ingredients set out like
#ingredients

Bolognese
bechemel
pasta
mozzarella
parmesan
This means they can be collected and shown by the Checklist plugin. This setup also works really well for keeping track of the Pantry, if I run out of something I just untick it from the list and it will automatically pop up when I go to buy the ingredients for some recipe...magic
